commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Nostr profile relay publish loop to retain and clear the per-relay timeout handle after the publish race settles.

PR surface: Source +8. Total +8 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main races `pool.publish` with a timeout promise but does not retain or clear that timeout handle when publish succeeds first. I did not run a live Nostr profile publish path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Keep open for contributor-provided real behavior proof and ordinary maintainer validation; there is no narrow ClawSweeper repair to queue on the PR branch.

target.title: fix: clear timeout timer in Nostr profile relay publish loop
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where each relay in the Nostr profile publish loop would leave a dangling timer after `Promise.race` completed, because the `setTimeout`-based timeout was never cleared when the relay publish succeeded.

## Why This Change Was Made

Wrap the per-relay `Promise.race` in a `try/finally` block that captures and clears the timer handle. Same pattern as the existing `withTimeout` helpers in the codebase.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Prevents a minor timer leak when publishing Nostr profiles to multiple relays.

## Evidence

- **`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile.test.ts`: 79/79 passed** ✅
- **`extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile.fuzz.test.ts`: all tests pass**
- Same cleanup pattern as `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts:37-45`
